Operation 'listPlayerMatches' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. The original description is too brief and lacks critical details for AI agent usability. It doesn't explain: (1) what the player ID parameter is or how to obtain it, (2) what query parameters are available for filtering/pagination, (3) the structure of the response (MatchListResponse), or (4) practical use cases. The improved version provides complete context about inputs, outputs, and use cases while maintaining consistency with the API's domain terminology (MatchListResponse, competitions, match objects).

data.current_description: Returns all matches of a player across all active competitions.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve all matches for a specific player
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all matches in which a specific player has participated across active competitions. Requires the player ID as a path parameter. Supports filtering and pagination through query parameters. Returns a MatchListResponse containing an array of match objects with details such as date, teams, scores, and competition information. Useful for viewing a player's match history and performance across different competitions.
data.reasoning: The original description is too brief and lacks critical details for AI agent usability. It doesn't explain: (1) what the player ID parameter is or how to obtain it, (2) what query parameters are available for filtering/pagination, (3) the structure of the response (MatchListResponse), or (4) practical use cases. The improved version provides complete context about inputs, outputs, and use cases while maintaining consistency with the API's domain terminology (MatchListResponse, competitions, match objects).

message: Operation 'listAreas' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and actionable, but description is completely missing. Added explanation of what areas represent, what the response contains, and use cases for AI agents to understand when to call this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all available geographic areas supported by the API. Returns a list of Area objects containing area identifiers, names, and country codes. Use this to discover available regions for filtering competitions and matches.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable, but description is completely missing. Added explanation of what areas represent, what the response contains, and use cases for AI agents to understand when to call this operation.

message: Operation 'getArea' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_input_details. Summary is clear but description is missing. Added details about the ID parameter requirement, response structure, and practical use case for AI agents to understand the relationship between this operation and listAreas.

data.current_summary: Get a specific area by ID
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves detailed information for a specific geographic area by its ID. Returns an Area object with the area's identifier, name, country code, and related metadata. Use this to get detailed information about a particular region after discovering it via listAreas.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear but description is missing. Added details about the ID parameter requirement, response structure, and practical use case for AI agents to understand the relationship between this operation and listAreas.

message: Operation 'listCompetitionMatches' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details. Summary is good but description is incomplete. The operation has 9 parameters but the description only mentions season default. Enhanced description to indicate filtering capabilities, pagination support, and response structure to help AI agents understand available filtering options and how to construct requests.

data.current_description: Returns all matches for a given competition. Defaults to the current season.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all matches for a specified competition, with optional filtering by season, status, and date range. Defaults to the current season if no season is specified. Returns a list of Match objects with scores, dates, and team information. Supports pagination and sorting parameters.
data.reasoning: Summary is good but description is incomplete. The operation has 9 parameters but the description only mentions season default. Enhanced description to indicate filtering capabilities, pagination support, and response structure to help AI agents understand available filtering options and how to construct requests.

message: Operation 'listCompetitionStandings' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details. Summary and core description are good, but missing details about available parameters and response structure. Enhanced to clarify that season can be optionally specified (not just current), and added information about the response format to help AI agents understand what data is available.

data.current_description: Returns the latest tables (total, home, away) for the current season of the given competition.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves league standings for a specified competition, including total, home, and away tables for the current season. Supports optional filtering by season and standings type. Returns a StandingsResponse containing team rankings, points, wins, losses, and other performance metrics.
data.reasoning: Summary and core description are good, but missing details about available parameters and response structure. Enhanced to clarify that season can be optionally specified (not just current), and added information about the response format to help AI agents understand what data is available.

message: Operation 'listMatches' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details. Original description lacks detail about available filter parameters and response structure. Enhanced description specifies supported filters (dateFrom, dateTo, status, competition), clarifies pagination, and explains what data is returned. This helps AI agents understand all available filtering options and construct appropriate requests.

data.current_description: Returns all matches for the current day by default. Use date filters for other dates.
data.description_suggestion: Returns matches across all competitions, filtered by date and status. Defaults to matches on the current day. Supports filtering by date range (dateFrom, dateTo), status (SCHEDULED, LIVE, FINISHED), and competition. Returns a paginated list of match objects with basic match information.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks detail about available filter parameters and response structure. data.description_suggestion: Returns a list of all teams available in the API. Supports optional filtering by area to retrieve teams from a specific geographic region. Returns team objects containing basic team information including name, area, and contact details.

data.description_suggestion: Returns all matches for a specific team, filtered by date and status. Supports filtering by date range (dateFrom, dateTo), match status (SCHEDULED, LIVE, FINISHED), and venue (HOME, AWAY). Returns a paginated list of match objects with match details including scores, dates, and opponent information.

Operation 'listPlayerMatches' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. The improved version provides complete context about inputs, outputs, and use cases while maintaining consistency with the API's domain terminology (MatchListResponse, competitions, match objects).

data.current_description: Returns all matches of a player across all active competitions.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve all matches for a specific player
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all matches in which a specific player has participated across active competitions. Requires the player ID as a path parameter. Supports filtering and pagination through query parameters. Returns a MatchListResponse containing an array of match objects with details such as date, teams, scores, and competition information. Useful for viewing a player's match history and performance across different competitions.
data.reasoning: The original description is too brief and lacks critical details for AI agent usability. It doesn't explain: (1) what the player ID parameter is or how to obtain it, (2) what query parameters are available for filtering/pagination, (3) the structure of the response (MatchListResponse), or (4) practical use cases. The improved version provides complete context about inputs, outputs, and use cases while maintaining consistency with the API's domain terminology (MatchListResponse, competitions, match objects).
